This looked fun, we love their game “I am cat” in my family. But after starting it up and doing the tutorial I was first given the option to spend real money on guns of all things. Confused I went on to play, I’m glad I played it before my family because, wow, this is a violent game disguised as a kids game. Don’t let the graphics or trailer fool you you. You play a vigilante bird who can literally shoot up a city. Like GTA, the police come after you when committing crimes (including murder) until you hide in a dumpster and they go away. On top of that the voices are done with hideously lazy AI and in broken English. Some scenes are just awful, you rescue other birds from people literally kicking them on the ground saying “taste pavement”. It’s also not fun. This game tries to rely on its idea of being funny but fails miserably. Also, why micro transactions? I’m not buying anymore games from this Dev, looks like they are just pumping out cheap crap for a quick buck.

I had a lot of fun on this game!! I would love for there to be more locations! Like imagine being able to explore the WHOLE WORLD. I know you probably couldn’t do the whole world. But adding a bit at a time could’ve REALLY cool! I definitely recommend this game! Especially if you like I Am Cat, I Am Monkey, or I Am Security!

“I Am Bird” is basically the game you would get if a wildlife documentary and a chaos gremlin had a baby. You play as a pigeon, seagull, or whatever feathered menace your heart desires, soaring over a lovely city with surprisingly gorgeous graphics. Dive-bombing pedestrians and blasting drones out of the sky has no right to be this fun.

But then the price tag lands.

For £15.99, I was expecting at least a dramatic cutscene, maybe a tragic backstory about a pigeon who lost his family to a rogue drone attack. Something. Instead, the game says, “Oh, you want golden wings or new weapons? That will be more money please.”

I mean, microtransactions in a game where my main goal is to bully people from 30 feet up? Let me have this one joy for free.

There is a great game underneath, but it sometimes feels like the developers are perched on a lamppost above me, waiting to swoop down and snatch a few extra coins.

Overall: A charming bird simulator with fun chaos, weighed down by wallet pecking paywalls.
Swords oh boy

It’s a really fun game i love  how you can fly and all but once you finish all the quests it does get boring after a while.

This is a very fun game but there are definitely some fundamental flaws preventing the game from being great (I know it's early access)

Pros:
-The flying feels great, is smooth, satisfying and you feel like you're in complete control
-Sound design- Hearing the wind pick up when you speed up, the conversations fade in and out as you fly low through the streets, the bustling sounds of the city as you're making your way around, all great
-Weapons- There is quite a variety of weapons and definitely some creative additions (for example: disco cannon that shoots disco balls at people, plays music and forces them to dance)
-The city design, while small, was really designed with thought behind making it fun to fly through. It's a ton of fun to soar through and swoop up or down through various things, straight toward a building and then swoop up and over it, etc.

Cons (hoping some of these will be addressed down the road):
-The walking is frustrating. Sometimes you just end up slipping when you try to walk
-Quest types have good variety, but you'll run out of them fast. A lot of quests can literally be completed in like 30 seconds or even less. Once you finish them all, you're done.
-There are sporadic rescue tasks that pop but it's literally just a matter of knocking out people that pick on a bird. Then you grab the bird's foot and hold it for a few seconds to rescue it
-Microtransactions (there are plenty of weapons you can get without microtansactions, and ways to get money in the game even after completing quests)
-AI voices- If it's a small team developing the game on a budget, I get it.
-Weapon handling is good when it works. But for some reason after extended play, the weapons don't line up right when I hold them while in the air
-The city is on the small side and there's only one map

If they could somehow implement a randomized task system and have more variety in the random encounters that pop up across the city, that alone would sky rocket the replay value.
